title: Changes in the money market
description: Practice what you have learned about how changes in the demand for money and/or the money supply affect the nominal interest rate in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
The current interest rate in Atlantis is $4\%$. At an interest rate of $4\%$, households want to hold $\$4$ million in cash and $\$2$ million in bonds. However, $M1$ is currently $\$3$ million.

**Which of the following best describes what will happen in the money market in Atlantis?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The nominal interest rate will increase, which will lower the quantity of money demanded
The nominal interest rate will increase, which will increase the quantity of money supplied
The nominal interest rate will decrease, which will lower the quantity of money demanded
The nominal interest rate will increase, which will lower the demand for money
The real interest rate will increase, which will increase the supply of money

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Maxistan is in a recession caused by a decrease in consumer confidence, as shown in the model here.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How will the change in the price level impact the money market and nominal interest rates?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane plots four labeled straight lines: A D subscript 1, A D subscript 2, S R A S, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled r G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects the horizontal axis at Y subscript f. Lines A D subscript 1 and A D subscript 2 are downward-sloping from top left to bottom right, with line A D subscript 2 to the left of A D subscript 1. Line S R A S is upward-sloping and intersects both A D curves. Line A D subscript 1 intersects S R A S at price level P L subscript 1 and output level Y subscript 1. Line A D subscript 2 intersects S R A S at price level P L subscript 2 and output level Y subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from P L subscript 1 and P L subscript 2 to the vertical axis. Vertical dashed lines drop from Y subscript 1 and Y subscript 2 to the horizontal axis.

choices.content: Demand for money decreases; nominal interest rates decrease
Demand for money increases; nominal interest rates increase
Supply and demand for money will both increase; the impact on nominal interest rates is indeterminate
Supply of money increases; nominal interest rates increase
Supply of money decreases; nominal interest rates increase

assessment_items.question_content:
Hamsterville’s national income increased due to an increase in government spending.

**How does an increase in national income affect the money market and interest rates?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Demand for money increases; interest rates increase
Demand for money increases; interest rates decrease
There is no impact on the money market; interest rates decrease
Supply of money increases; interest rates decreases
Supply of money increases; interest rates increases

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following would cause an unambiguous decrease in nominal interest rates?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in the price level
A decrease in the monetary base
Households sell bonds
People are able to access money more easily
Aggregate demand increases

assessment_items.question_content:
**All of the following lead to an increase in nominal interest rates EXCEPT**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The central bank buys bonds.
There is a higher aggregate price level.
There are new regulations limiting how many times people can withdraw money.
The central bank raises the reserve requirement.
Real GDP increases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**If there is an increase in aggregate demand, what happens in the money market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The demand for money increases and nominal interest rates increase.
The demand for money decreases and nominal interest rates decrease.
The market for money is unaffected.
The supply of money increases and nominal interest rates decrease.
The supply of money decreases and nominal interest rates increase.

assessment_items.question_content:
**If a new technology is invented that makes it easier to withdraw money from the bank, which of the following will occur in the money market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The demand for money decreases and the nominal interest rate decreases.
The demand for money increases and the nominal interest rate decreases.
The demand for money decreases and the nominal interest rate increases.
The demand for money increases and the nominal interest rate increases.
The demand for money increases and the nominal interest rate doesn’t change.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following increases the demand for money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a higher price level
a higher nominal interest rate
a lower nominal interest rate
the central bank buys bonds
the central bank lowers the discount rate

assessment_items.question_content:
**If there is rapid inflation throughout the economy, which of the following is the most likely result?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The demand for money increases and the nominal interest rate increases.
The demand for money decreases and the nominal interest rate increases.
The money supply increases and the nominal interest rate increases.
The money supply increases and the nominal interest rate decreases.
The demand for money decreases and the nominal interest rate decreases.

assessment_items.question_content:
The development of online banking has made it easier for people to move wealth to cash from other forms of assets. 

**What change would counteract the effect of this on the nominal interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A decrease in the money supply.
There is lower inflation.
Banks reduce their holdings of excess reserves.
The demand for money decreases.
The central bank lowers the reserve requirement.

assessment_items.question_content:
**All of the following decrease nominal interest rates EXCEPT:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: People start keeping more money outside of banks
Reducing the reserve requirement.
Banks reduce their holdings of excess reserves.
The aggregate price level decreases.
Improvements in banking technology

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following decreases the money supply?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: People keep money in their homes instead of banks.
The reserve requirement decreases.
Banks loan more and keep less in excess reserves.
The aggregate price level decreases.
People prefer to hold more money, regardless of the interest rate.
